front: Movement: experimental liberal arts college near Asheville, North Carolina, founded in 1933 after the Nazis shut down the Bauhaus
Movement: existed for 23 years; John Andrew Rice was its first rector
Movement: Josef Albers taught a Werklehre class emphasizing 'fingertip touch for materials'; Tom Wolfe mockingly described paper exercises
Movement: Josef Albers wrote Interaction of Color, a textbook on color theory; discussed Bezout Effect
Movement: Homage to the Square: three or four nested quadrangles, palette knife on Masonite, entries include Ascending and Skyward
Movement: Anni Albers was first textile designer with solo MoMA exhibition (1949); wrote On Weaving; created Six Prayers for Holocaust victims
Movement: Anni forbade Josef from selling any painting using red because red represented love and passion
back: Black Mountain College (Josef and Anni Albers)

front: Movement: site of Theater Piece No. 1, a happening by John Cage; Rauschenberg's White Paintings installed on ceiling
Movement: Robert Rauschenberg, known for combines, was a student; sat onstage painting during Cunningham's dance Story
Movement: John Cage rediscovered Satie's Vexations here and organized 840-repetition premiere with David Tudor
Movement: Ruth Asawa created looped-wire hangings after watching basket makers in Toluca, Mexico
Movement: Susan Weil introduced her future husband Robert Rauschenberg to photograms at this college
Movement: Merce Cunningham founded his dance company here in 1953; partnered with Cage
Movement: Willem de Kooning painted Asheville and Excavation inspired by his time here
Movement: Cy Twombly attended and said it was impossible not to love Rilke afterward
Movement: William Carlos Williams was a noteworthy lecturer at this college
Movement: Charles Olson pioneered 'projective verse' and 'composition by field'; wrote Call Me Ishmael and The Maximus Poems
Movement: Robert Creeley wrote 'I Know a Man'; Denise Levertov, Ed Dorn, Robert Duncan also associated; called 'projectivists'
Movement: Philip Johnson offered Josef Albers head of painting position here
Movement: Walter Gropius had Josef Albers create mural Manhattan for the Pan Am Building lobby
